2606.31518.title: Design and Implementation of Agentic Orchestrations and Orchestration of Agents
2606.31518.paper_summary: Agentic Business Process Management has gained momentum recently. The prospect is that the autonomy of AI agents, i.e., predominantly LLM-based agents, can be balanced with a certain level of robustness, tractability, and traceability through a combination with process technology. In this paper, we provide a classification framework for agentic orchestration options along properties such as task specificity, traceability and tractability, autonomy and reactivity, and correctness assurance and present qualitative decision criteria for realizations of different scenarios. We also provide metrics for the quantitative assessment of realization properties and show them through different agentic implementations of a predictive light sensing scenario. Altogether, this work aims at providing properties, criteria, and metrics for the design and implementation of agentic orchestrations and orchestration of agents.
2606.31518.paper_summary_zh: 代理商業流程管理最近獲得了動力。前景是，AI 代理的自主性，即主要基於 LLM 的代理，可以通過與流程技術的結合，在一定程度的穩健性、可處理性和可追溯性之間取得平衡。在本文中，我們提供了一個針對代理協調選項的分類框架，涵蓋任務特異性、可追溯性和可處理性、自主性和反應性以及正確性保證等屬性，並提出了不同場景實現的定性決策標準。我們還提供了用於定量評估實現屬性的指標，並通過不同的代理實現預測光感知場景來展示這些指標。總體而言，這項工作旨在為代理協調及代理的協調設計和實施提供屬性、標準和指標。
2606.31518.author: Stefanie Rinderle-Ma et.al.
2606.31518.authors: Stefanie Rinderle-Ma, Juergen Mangler, Johannes Loebbecke, Dominik Voigt, Nataliia Klievtsova, Matthias Ehrendorfer
